payload.blocker: Specification conflict: Two contradictory coverage calculation formulas
payload.details:
The architecture document contains two different formulas for coverage calculation:

1. ARCH-002 (line 53): coverage = (answered / total) * 100
   - Simple ratio formula
   - All questions weighted equally
   
2. ARCH-005 (lines 180-182): Penalty-based weights
   - Critical unanswered: -15% each
   - Important unanswered: -10% each
   - Optional unanswered: -5% each
   - Formula: coverage = 100% - sum(unanswered_penalties)

The tests appear to mix both approaches:
- TestCalculateGap_AllAnswered expects 100% (works with either formula)
- TestCalculateGap_NoneAnswered expects 0% (only works with ratio formula)
- TestCalculateGap_CriticalUnanswered expects 85% (only works with penalty formula)
- TestCalculateGap_MixedPriorities expects 60% (only works with penalty formula)

With penalty formula and NoneAnswered test data (2C, 1I, 1O):
- Expected: 0%
- Actual: 100% - 30% - 10% - 5% = 55%

With ratio formula and CriticalUnanswered test data (2C, 1 answered):
- Expected: 85%
- Actual: 50%

No single formula satisfies all test expectations.

evidence.content:
Critical unanswered: -15% coverage each
Important unanswered: -10% coverage each
Optional unanswered: -5% coverage each
Expects 85% with 1 of 2 critical answered (penalty formula: 100% - 15%)

payload.suggested_resolution:
Clarify which formula is correct, or specify if a hybrid approach is intended. Options:

Option A: Pure penalty formula (ARCH-005)
- Fix TestCalculateGap_NoneAnswered to expect 55% instead of 0%
- Or change test data to have enough questions that all unanswered = 100% penalty

Option B: Pure ratio formula (ARCH-002)
- Remove priority weights from tests
- Fix all tests expecting weighted behavior
- Update ARCH-005 to remove contradictory weights

Option C: Hybrid formula
- Define clearly how ratio and penalties combine
- Update both architecture docs and tests to match
- Example: base = ratio, then apply penalties: coverage = (answered/total * 100) - unanswered_penalties

previous_phases.RED: Complete with 19 tests (3 passing property tests, 16 failing example tests)
